title: Cricket - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Cricket Mhatre’s hamstring blow deepens Chennai’s IPL crisis By Reuters The 18-year-old has been the most prolific batter for five-time champions Chennai, who dropped to seventh place in the 10-team league after Saturday’s defeat by Sunrisers Hyderabad. How the US could clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz By US deploys drones and robotic systems to clear Hormuz mines, but operation remains slow
content:
Cricket Mhatre’s hamstring blow deepens Chennai’s IPL crisis By Reuters The 18-year-old has been the most prolific batter for five-time champions Chennai, who dropped to seventh place in the 10-team league after Saturday’s defeat by Sunrisers Hyderabad. How the US could clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz By US deploys drones and robotic systems to clear Hormuz mines, but operation remains slow and exposed to Iranian threats. Airee to lead Nepal T20Is, Paudel retains ODI captaincy By Binod Pandey CAN split leadership roles for UAE T20Is and League 2 tri-series against UAE and Oman. Nepali cricketers continue to raise questions about team selection process By Nayak Paudel On Tuesday, Karnali’s star pacer Anuj Chanara quit the team citing unfair selection process. This was only the latest in a series of controversies over transparency in both domestic teams and the national setup. Aasif Sheikh’s double century steals the spotlight at Men’s PM Cup By Nayak Paudel The eighth edition of the Men’s PM Cup marked the tournament’s first-ever double century on Monday. The day also saw the most sixes in an innings and the highest totals. Aasif Sheikh becomes first player to hit double hundred in domestic cricket By Binod Pandey The opener struck 208 off 142 balls in the PM Cup, breaking Pradeep Airee’s long-standing record. Karan KC banned for one match over social media post targeting umpires By Post Report CAN also fines Nepal Police Club bowler full match fee after remark deemed against spirit of the game. Umpires’ association demands strict action against Karan KC By Nayak Paudel KC commented on a social media post on Saturday seeking the number of matches a player would be banned for if he mistakenly beats an umpire. More players to get exposed to longer format after CAN expands Jay Trophy By Nayak Paudel The expansion of Nepal’s only multi-day tournament from four to six teams is expected to provide greater and higher-quality exposure for emerging talents. Can the Rhinos repeat another miraculous comeback in League 2? By Nayak Paudel With Nepal once more near the bottom of the ICC CWC League 2 table, the Rhinos must replicate the 2023 miracle run to keep maiden Cricket World Cup qualification hopes alive. APF end Sudurpashchim’s winning streak By Nayak Paudel Despite the loss, Sudurpashchim stay firm in the second position, with 10 points from five wins in seven games. 2nd NPL’s total gross revenue over Rs473 million By Post Report CAN’s board meeting on Monday approved the income and expenditure during the 2nd NPL. The meeting also decided to use ICC’s funding of USD500,000 in women’s cricket. Could Arjun Kumal be the next big thing in Nepali cricket? By Nayak Paudel Kumal, perhaps the most consistent Nepali batter of late, smashed a century on Saturday and became the fastest to reach 1,000 runs in the tournament. He now awaits a senior national team debut. Gaurishankar Super League T20 for U17 boys from April 12 By Post Report The 18 academies, including a team from the organiser, are being divided into six groups of three each. Madhesh top table at Men’s PM Cup By Post Report Madhesh and Army have seven points in four games. Madhesh have bested Army in net run rate. Koshi’s losing streak at PM Cup continues By Post Report The easternmost province suffered a seven-wicket defeat against Sudurpashchim on Friday. Madhesh bested Lumbini in the other match of the day. Kantipur Max to broadcast IPL By Post Report Xtreme Energy Drinks signs on as title sponsor; Laminar Tiles and Mangalam Pipes join as sponsors. Dev Khanal shines as Lumbini beat Koshi by 148 runs By Post Report Sudurpaschim, powered by a six-wicket haul by Hemant Dhami, defeat Karnali by four wickets in the other match of the Men’s PM Cup on Monday. Teams share points as rain washes out two openers of Men’s PM Cup By Post Report There are 45 league-stage matches in the tournament between 10 teams. The top two teams will play the final in Birgunj on April 17. Rampant India complete cricket T20 World Cup treble, NZ fall short again By Reuters The victory will taste particularly sweet for India since it came at a venue where they were beaten by Australia in the final of the 50-overs World Cup three years ago.
